source: Original — Mörk Borg Module Runner sensory bank. Short, stackable beats in the canonical voice (smell + sound + light + touch + ambient detail), used to layer atmosphere on generated nodes and to roll fresh details at the table on demand.
smells: Wet wool and somebody's sickness.
Copper, sharp and recent.
Old smoke soaked into the stone.
Lily-rot, sweet and apologetic.
Sweat without recent heat.
Ash drifting from a fire that is over.
Cold iron and older blood.
Low tide where there should be no tide.
Bread that has forgotten what bread is.
Fox-piss and damp moss.
Gunpowder from a war nobody admits to.
The inside of a coffin lid — that particular wood.
Vinegar with a sweetness underneath.
Tallow, badly rendered.
River-mud and something rusted.
A faint sweetness like old funeral flowers.
Urine, but not from any animal you'd want to meet.
Wet feathers.
A cellar that has been opened too soon.
Burnt hair and the prayer that went with it.
A stable that hasn't held a horse in years.
Salt-crust and slow rot.
Something sweet on the tongue, not in the air.
Ozone and turned milk.
The back of a butcher's shop, after hours.
The faint resin of a green wound.
Damp wool and grave-soil, mixed.
Candles guttered out hours ago.
The air just after a confession.
Winter trying to remember itself.
A church that nobody has prayed in for a long time.
Hot bread coming from a house with no chimney.
sounds: A distant bell that doesn't ring on the hour.
Dripping, but into something soft.
Scratching from behind a wall.
A hymn sung off-key in another room.
The click of a door that wasn't open.
Breathing that is not yours.
The wet crunch of stepped-on insects.
A kettle whistling in a house that doesn't have one.
Wind across an opening that isn't a chimney.
A child's footsteps, fast, going nowhere.
A string section in your skull tuning up.
The very faint rustle of pages being turned.
A long exhale from a room you can't find.
The scrape of metal on stone, slow, patient.
A single hammer-blow every minute or so.
Mice that aren't quite mice.
One voice praying in a dead language.
The crack of frozen wood in summer.
A cough that is too dry to be human.
A clock ticking that you cannot find.
A knife being sharpened, methodically.
Soft laughter, around a corner you didn't take.
A knocking — three times, then four, then three.
Chains being lowered, somewhere, slowly.
A sigh from someone too tired to make one.
The snap of fingers, very close.
Water that is moving uphill.
A song you almost recognise, sung wrong.
The small precise sound of a beetle in distress.
A wet smacking that is much too rhythmic.
The faint, regular toll of a bell that was buried.
A door closing in a corridor that has no doors.
lights: Light the colour of an unhealing wound.
Lantern-yellow that doesn't reach the corners.
A watery grey, like dishwater.
The wrong noon — too bright, the wrong angle.
Candlelight that flickers without a draft.
Moonlight the colour of sour milk.
Light that arrives a moment late.
A colour you cannot name afterward.
The green of old bruises.
A thin red glow with no source.
The kind of pale that makes faces ugly.
Dim blue, like a dawn that hasn't been earned.
Light the way it looks behind closed eyelids.
A torch-flame that bends the wrong way.
A window-shaped patch of brightness on a wall with no window.
The white of bone, giving off no warmth.
Shadows that fall toward the centre of the room.
A light that feels watched.
The dull glow of something that should not still be hot.
The dim, decisive light of a court at sentencing.
tactile: The air sticks to you in slow, unwelcome layers.
Cold that settles in the bones, not the skin.
A feeling of being too tall for this room.
The persistent sense of being watched from below.
The floor gives slightly underfoot, like it isn't quite floor.
A tightness in the throat that arrived with you.
The walls feel closer when you turn around.
Something brushed against you, then nothing.
A damp that finds its way under clothes.
Skin prickling for no weather reason.
A pressure on the temples, mild but present.
A faint vibration in the teeth.
Warmth from no source, in just one corner.
A slight, persistent tug, in a direction you don't want to look.
motes: Three crows on the gable, watching the same nothing.
A hand-mark on the window — palm-out, on the wrong side.
Dried wax in a shape that wasn't poured.
A door left ajar with no draft to ajar it.
Footprints leading away that lead nowhere.
A single feather, lying perfectly centred.
Chalk-marks at every threshold, faded.
A stain on the floor in roughly the shape of a body.
Coins arranged in a small pile, deliberately.
A chair pulled out, awaiting a guest.
A clock stopped at a time that hasn't happened yet.
A wreath of dried grass hung above the door.
Water-stains in the ceiling in the shape of a face.
A child's doll, seated in an adult chair.
Ash on the floor, around the boundary of a chalk circle that has been swept up.
A wineglass with one perfect lip-print on it, still wet.
A folded coat hung on a hook nobody put there.
A single shoe by the door, the right size for nobody here.
A stack of unread letters, all addressed to the same name.
The page in the open book is dated tomorrow.
A single warm coin, on the windowsill.
A small bowl of milk, untouched, far from any cat.
